channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: As more people turn to chatbots for health advice, studies say they may be led astray
item.description: People are turning to AI chatbots to help them with medical advice. Recent studies suggest these bots are not always helpful in making decisions about...

item.title: Federal Aid for Lead Cleanup Is Receding. That’s a Problem for Cash-Strapped Cities.
item.description: Congress and the Trump administration are rolling back some lead remediation resources. Case studies of two cities and a state that faced lead contami...

item.title: Even Patients Are Shocked by the Prices Their Insurers Will Pay — And It Costs All of Us
item.description: Health care prices are on the rise, and patients are flummoxed that even insurance companies aren’t doing more to control costs.

item.title: Readers Lean On Congress To Solve Crises in Research and Rehab
item.description: KFF Health News gives readers a chance to comment on a recent batch of stories.

item.title: Got elbow or heel pain? Shockwave therapy can help
item.description: As more people stay active as they age, overuse injuries are increasing. A non-invasive treatment known as shockwave therapy can help ease heel and el...

item.title: Some families afraid of ICE are forgoing medical care. These volunteers help them
item.description: In Minneapolis, underground healthcare networks of volunteer doctors and nurses bring care into the homes of families too scared of immigration enforc...

item.title: A homeless shelter and rehab center holds a marathon to get people back on their feet
item.description: Outside of Chicago, a homeless shelter and rehab center called Wayside Cross hosts a unique program to help people get back on their feet, one mile at...

item.title: Ivermectin is making a post-pandemic comeback, among cancer patients
item.description: The anti-parasitic drug became a household name during the COVID-19 pandemic, and it is now being embraced as an alternative treatment for cancer. It ...

item.title: Families Defend Disability Services Amid Medicaid Cuts
item.description: Idaho is positioning to slash Medicaid funding as state lawmakers grapple with the effects of the federal One Big Beautiful Bill Act, which President ...

item.title: Medicaid Is Paying for More Dental Care. GOP Cuts Threaten To Reverse the Trend.
item.description: More than three dozen states cover dental services for low-income and disabled individuals on Medicaid, in recognition of such care’s importance to ov...

item.title: Miriam Paramore, RxUtility
item.description: In this quickbite interview recorded at the Feb 2026 VIVE conference, I am talking with Miriam Paramore. Miriam is building RxUtility, which is helpin...

item.title: The Vocabulary of Survival
item.description: By GEORGE BEAUREGARD From 2018 to 2022, I served as a physician executive in a large health system on Long Island. During that period, I became acquai...

item.title: Will AI Solve Immunology’s Debate Over “Self vs. Non-Self?”
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE In 1872, English mathematician and sometimes poet, Augustus de Morgan, wrote this catching rhyme: “Great fleas have little fleas upon th...

item.title: Can We Ride the GenAI Wave Without Getting Subsumed by It?
item.description: By DAVID SHAYWITZ “There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen,” said Lenin, probably never. It’s also a remarka...

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...
